title: Moira Rosé

jokes.text: David asking 'Who came up with the word "bunt"? Sounds like something you'd need to see a surgeon for.'
jokes.setup: Patrick explains baseball terminology
jokes.punchline: David finds medical connotations in innocent sports term
jokes.explanation: Strong character comedy - David's privileged perspective finding innuendo in innocent terms. Well-structured with clear setup/payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: It's just a figure of speech. 'Batting cleanup' means you're the fourth batter in the lineup.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, that's a horrible name for a position. It sounds like you're being treated for a sexually transmitted infection.

jokes.text: David: 'I was referring to the pizza. But, also, the game.'
jokes.setup: David claimed he could 'get into' the baseball game
jokes.punchline: Reveals his priorities are food-focused with perfunctory interest in sports
jokes.explanation: Efficient character moment showing David's superficial engagement with Patrick's interests.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm going to get us some overpriced concession stand hot dogs, and then we can leave after the first inning.

jokes.text: Moira's entrance: 'What do we have here? I seem to have stumbled into some sort of gentlemen's cavern.'
jokes.setup: Moira enters motel room where men are watching sports
jokes.punchline: Overwrought description of simple scene using theatrical language
jokes.explanation: Pure Moira - transforms mundane observation into theatrical pronouncement. 'Gentlemen's cavern' is perfectly pompous.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Good heavens! What vulgar pageantry unfolds before my eyes! These... these electronic transmissions of grown men in tight pantaloons, grappling for an oblong spheroid whilst a studio audience of thousands looks on with bated breath!

jokes.text: When David told me he wanted to install a Japanese toilet at my place, he neglected to mention that it would involve taking out a wall.
jokes.setup: Patrick explains why he's staying overnight
jokes.punchline: David's characteristically over-the-top renovation project
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures David's tendency to overcomplicate simple things, while Patrick's dry delivery makes it funnier

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I'm staying overnight because David's doing some work on the apartment.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's not just 'some work.' I'm completely gutting the bedroom. I've hired a designer, a contractor, and a feng shui consultant. We're removing the walls, relocating the windows, and I'm having the floors imported from Italy.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: For a bedroom renovation?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Patrick, if we're going to do this, we're going to do it right. I'm not going to live in some mediocre bedroom. That's not who I am.

jokes.text: The 'Moira Rosé' pun and David's deadpan reaction
jokes.setup: Herb wants to name wine after Moira
jokes.punchline: Obvious pun on Moira's name + David's lack of enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Predictable pun that everyone sees coming, saved slightly by David's unenthusiastic response.

dialogue.speaker: Herb
dialogue.line: I want to name a wine after you, Moira. I'm thinking 'Moira Noir'.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh, Herb, that's divine!

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's not.

jokes.text: They should call it a... a Moira Rosé. [Chuckles] - Yes, that's what he has in mind.
jokes.setup: David suggests a wine name playing on his mother's name
jokes.punchline: Moira's deflating response that it's already the plan
jokes.explanation: Nice pun but the deflation when Moira reveals it's already planned adds character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: We could name a wine after you. Something like 'Moira's Vineyard' or—

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Yes, we've already decided on that.

jokes.text: [Faux excitement] Mm. What news!
jokes.setup: Patrick congratulates Moira enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: David's clearly fake enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: David's inability to hide his lack of excitement about his mother's projects. Economy is excellent.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's great, Mom. Really great. I'm so happy for you.

jokes.text: David's obviously rehearsed excuse sequence with Patrick
jokes.setup: David claims he can't go to wine tasting
jokes.punchline: Patrick calls out their obviously scripted interaction
jokes.explanation: Meta-comedy acknowledging the artificial nature of the setup. Patrick's 'rehearse more next time' is perfectly delivered.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I can't go to the wine tasting.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Why not?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I have a prior commitment.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: David, we literally just made plans five minutes ago.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, my commitment is very prior.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm sensing some resistance here. Are you sure you don't want to go?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm positive.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Because it's going to be really fun. There's going to be wine, and cheese...

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Patrick, are we doing a bit right now?

jokes.text: You guys might wanna rehearse a little more next time. The extraction did feel a bit strained. - [Quietly] That was you. - Wha?
jokes.setup: Patrick comments on how forced their excuse-making seemed
jokes.punchline: David quietly blaming Patrick, then his confused response
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on their own awkward performance, with David's defensive whisper and confusion adding layers.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, maybe if someone hadn't insisted on lying about having a sick aunt, we wouldn't be in this mess.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Your aunt is sick?

jokes.text: Alexis's cult recruitment language: 'are you ready for a total physical and emotional transformation?'
jokes.setup: Alexis promotes fitness class in over-the-top language
jokes.punchline: Language is clearly cult-like recruitment speak
jokes.explanation: Sets up cult storyline with appropriately suspicious language that Alexis misses.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Okay, so I'm starting this amazing new fitness class, and honestly, it's going to change your life. Like, completely transform you. You'll be reborn. You'll shed your old self like a snake sheds its skin, except better because you'll also have abs.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: And once you join, you can never leave. I mean, you *can*, but you won't *want* to. Everyone who's ever done one class comes back. They have to. It's like a spiritual awakening, but with burpees.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You'll give me all your money, obviously. Not for the class—well, for the class—but also just because you'll want to. You'll want to give me everything. That's what happens when you find your purpose.

jokes.text: Stevie: 'Nah, but I'll do your class.'
jokes.setup: Asked if ready for transformation
jokes.punchline: Completely deflates enthusiasm while agreeing to participate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan - maximum deflation with minimum words. Excellent economy.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah, sure. I'm ready.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Let's transform me into... whatever this is.

jokes.text: Well, you pushed pretty hard for it, Alexis. - You said you'd buy me lunch.
jokes.setup: Alexis acts like people came willingly to her event
jokes.punchline: Stevie reveals the real motivation was free food
jokes.explanation: Stevie's dry honesty deflating Alexis's enthusiasm. Good character work showing their dynamic.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Everyone was so excited to come to my event. It was literally the most popular thing I've ever organized.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Alexis, the only reason anyone showed up is because you said there would be free food.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, yes, but they still came.

jokes.text: I kinda wish I knew that before I spent all that money on my LuLu Limes.
jokes.setup: Discussion about having to change out of workout clothes
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's mispronunciation of 'Lululemon' as 'LuLu Limes'
jokes.explanation: Classic Jocelyn malapropism - shows her character through innocent mispronunciation of trendy brand.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: I need to change out of my LuLu Limes.

jokes.text: Yeah, I kinda wish I knew that before I spent all that money on my LuLu Limes.
jokes.setup: Learning they have to change out of their workout clothes
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's mispronunciation of Lululemon as 'LuLu Limes'
jokes.explanation: Jocelyn's malapropism is both character-appropriate and genuinely funny - 'LuLu Limes' sounds like a fruit brand

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Wait, we have to change out of our LuLu Limes?

jokes.text: How does he know all of our names? - Am I crazy or is he really cute?
jokes.setup: Stevie notices something creepy about Citrus knowing their names
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn is completely distracted by finding him attractive
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Stevie's suspicious awareness and Jocelyn's oblivious attraction. Sets up cult danger.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: That's creepy. How does he know our names?

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: I don't know, but have you seen how attractive he is?

jokes.text: Nah. - What if I just wait in the car until you're ready for lunch? 'Cause I'm just getting a really weird vibe from this place.
jokes.setup: Asked if they're ready to elevate
jokes.punchline: Ronnie's flat rejection and Stevie wanting to escape
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moments - Ronnie's bluntness and Stevie's desire to avoid the weird situation.

dialogue.speaker: Ronnie
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I want to leave.

jokes.text: What if I just wait in the car until you're ready for lunch?
jokes.setup: The cultish atmosphere of the class
jokes.punchline: Stevie's practical escape plan
jokes.explanation: Stevie cutting straight to her exit strategy while everyone else gets swept up

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I have a practical escape plan. I'm going to wait until they're all in the meditation circle, then I'm just going to walk out to my car and leave.

jokes.text: Stevie's weak 'oh yeah' response to cult enthusiasm
jokes.setup: Enthusiastic group chant
jokes.punchline: Stevie's halfhearted participation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat - Stevie's reluctance expressed through minimal effort.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah... okay.

jokes.text: Johnny: 'Okay, not off to a good start, Roland.'
jokes.setup: Roland's inappropriate bedroom confession
jokes.punchline: Johnny's understated response to obvious boundary violation
jokes.explanation: Johnny's reasonable response to Roland's inappropriateness creates good contrast.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm going to need you to never say anything like that to me again.

jokes.text: Maybe Patrick should get 'the talk' from old Uncle Roland.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Johnny talking to Patrick
jokes.punchline: Roland volunteering for the inappropriate task
jokes.explanation: Roland's complete lack of self-awareness about why he'd be the wrong person for this

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I'll do it. I'll talk to Patrick.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: What? No, Roland, you can't—

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Why not? I'm very good with people. I once talked a man out of a timeshare.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: That's not the same thing.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, I'm volunteering anyway. Consider it my civic duty.

jokes.text: 'Spines tend to liquidate in the presence of success'
jokes.setup: Moira explains why people will want to work with her now
jokes.punchline: Overwrought metaphor about people losing backbone around successful people
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira vocabulary - takes simple concept and makes it needlessly elaborate and poetic.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: People lose their backbone around successful people. It's like watching a school of fish scatter when a shark enters the room. Except the shark is me, and I'm fabulous.

jokes.text: [Quietly] ERT-linger. ERT. LINGER. - Herb Ertlinger. - Nailed it first try.
jokes.setup: David coaching Moira on pronunciation
jokes.punchline: His proud claim about nailing it after clearly practicing
jokes.explanation: David's false confidence after clearly needing help. Shows his relationship with his mother and need to appear competent.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You're saying it wrong. It's 'croissant,' not 'crescent.'

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I've been saying it that way for years.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, and you've been saying it wrong for years. Here, listen to me. Croissant.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Croissant.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: No, croissant.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: That's what I said. Croissant.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You know what, I've been practicing this in the mirror for twenty minutes, and I think I've nailed it.

jokes.text: Moira's reaction to wine label: 'Well, hello! Who is she and how do we get a mouthful of her?!'
jokes.setup: Herb shows wine label with Moira's image
jokes.punchline: Moira sexualizes her own image on wine label
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Moira's narcissism and dramatic flair combining in unexpected way.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh my God, is that me? That's a very sensual photograph. I'm looking at myself like I want to take myself out to dinner.

jokes.text: David's deadpan 'Rich in potassium' about banana rosé
jokes.setup: Herb offers banana rosé wine
jokes.punchline: David responds with nutritional fact instead of wine critique
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of wine pretension through practical observation.

dialogue.speaker: Herb
dialogue.line: I brought a banana rosé. It's my own recipe.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: A banana has 422 milligrams of potassium.

jokes.text: Wine tasting disaster - 'tastes like Amoxicillin' and 'burning my throat'
jokes.setup: Mother and son taste wines to find the right one
jokes.punchline: All wines are terrible in specific, visceral ways
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy through taste reactions, builds tension for eventual drunk scene.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: This one tastes like a tire fire had a baby with a port-a-potty.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: This one tastes like someone's describing a wine they've never actually tasted.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: This one tastes like regret and wood chips.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: This one tastes like someone sneezed into a bottle of sadness.

jokes.text: Johnny calling pizza 'za' and immediately regretting it
jokes.setup: Johnny tries to sound casual about pizza
jokes.punchline: Uses obviously forced slang that hangs in the air awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - middle-aged man trying too hard to be cool.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: So, uh, we're just gonna grab some... za? You know, keep it chill, no cap, it's giving carbs.

jokes.text: Uh... yeah, I could go for some 'za. [Chuckles]
jokes.setup: Patrick offers pizza
jokes.punchline: Johnny trying to sound casual with 'za' and immediately laughing at himself
jokes.explanation: Johnny's awkward attempt to be cool/casual with slang, immediately undermined by his own nervous laughter.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I ordered some 'za.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: 'Za?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: *laughs* I'm sorry, I can't—I can't say that.

jokes.text: Oh, that's okay, cold pizza can be good. - Mm. - Hot pizza's good. Too-hot pizza - not so good. I got that happening.
jokes.setup: Normal conversation about pizza temperature
jokes.punchline: Johnny's increasingly unnecessary commentary showing his nervousness
jokes.explanation: Shows Johnny's nervousness through over-explaining obvious things. Builds awkward father-son-in-law tension.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Is the pizza hot?

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Yes, it's fresh out of the oven.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Hot. Yes. Very hot. Piping hot, one might say. The kind of hot that requires caution. Extreme caution.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: It's pizza, Johnny.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, well, I'm simply noting the thermal properties. And the heat. The considerable heat. Did I mention it's hot?

jokes.text: Yeah, cheese, tomato sauce, bread. Can't beat that combination.
jokes.setup: Continuing pizza conversation
jokes.punchline: Johnny stating the absolute basics of pizza as if profound
jokes.explanation: Johnny's desperate attempt to keep conversation going leads to stating the obvious about pizza ingredients.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny Rose
dialogue.line: Pizza is a circle. It comes in slices. You eat the slices. That's pizza.

jokes.text: Okay, yeah, I didn't hate it, but I mean, does anybody even know what 'the gateway' is?
jokes.setup: Asked about her experience with the class
jokes.punchline: Stevie's reluctant admission followed by the obvious question everyone's avoiding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie - cutting through the BS to ask the obvious question everyone else is avoiding. Great straight-man moment.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: It was... fine. I learned some things.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: What things?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I don't know, things about acting. Look, can we please not do this?

jokes.text: Gateway clarification: 'It's a symbol... But it's also a literal gateway into the sky'
jokes.setup: Alexis asks for clarification about symbolic gateway
jokes.punchline: Citrus confirms it's both metaphor AND literal sky portal
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure - gives reasonable explanation then immediately undercuts it with absurd truth.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: So you're saying it's a metaphor?

dialogue.speaker: Citrus
dialogue.line: It's both a metaphor AND a literal sky portal.

jokes.text: Alexis's cult definition: 'not the good kind... not like when you go to India to follow a long-haired, sexy man around'
jokes.setup: Alexis realizes they've joined a cult
jokes.punchline: Distinguishes between 'bad' cults and her personal cult experiences
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - only Alexis would rank cults by personal benefit and attractiveness of leaders.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I mean, okay, so yes, technically we were in a cult, but it wasn't like a *bad* cult. It was more of a... lifestyle choice with some unconventional beliefs and mandatory financial contributions.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: And also, I've been in way worse cults than that one. That one was actually kind of fun.

jokes.text: Jocelyn's Ikea meatball confession
jokes.setup: Jocelyn explains why she wants to go on cult trip
jokes.punchline: Reveals desperate need for alone time through specific, sad example
jokes.explanation: Perfectly specific and sad detail that makes cult trip seem appealing by comparison.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: I just need to get away. My husband won't stop talking to me. Yesterday, he followed me into the bathroom and talked to me through the door for twenty minutes about his fantasy football league.

jokes.text: Oh my gosh, did you not know that? My mom tried to take me to the gateway for spring break one year. I just thought I was supporting your career.
jokes.setup: Everyone acting shocked about the cult revelation
jokes.punchline: Twyla casually revealing she knew all along and has family cult history
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - Twyla's innocent sweetness hiding dark family history. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Oh, I knew. My family's been in cults for generations. My mother was in one, my grandmother was in one. It's kind of a thing.

jokes.text: 'Cults prey on sad, weak, vulnerable people' followed by 'I'll try not to take that personally'
jokes.setup: Alexis explains how cults work
jokes.punchline: Stevie points out Alexis just described the people she recruited
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/punchline with perfect Stevie deadpan delivery calling out the obvious implication.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Okay, so cults work by making you feel like you're part of something special and exclusive, and then they slowly isolate you from your friends and family, and before you know it, you're donating all your money and sleeping in a compound.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: So basically, you just described your MLM downline.

jokes.text: Exit sign pointing straight up
jokes.setup: Looking for emergency exit from cult building
jokes.punchline: Exit sign literally points toward 'gateway' in sky
jokes.explanation: Clever visual gag that reinforces cult's literal sky-gateway belief.

dialogue.line: Exit sign literally points toward 'gateway' in sky

jokes.text: David's wine pun attempts: 'Moira-lot' and 'Moira Rose-ling'
jokes.setup: David tries to create wine name puns
jokes.punchline: Forced wine puns that don't quite work
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad puns that show David trying too hard to be helpful.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I've been working on some wine names. There's 'Pinot Grigio-away,' 'Chardonnay the Bodyguard,' and my personal favorite, 'Riesling and Shine.'

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: What? They're clever.

jokes.text: 'I have had worst things in my mouth'
jokes.setup: Moira tastes terrible wine blend
jokes.punchline: Ominous comment about her past experiences
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - makes audience wonder about her mysterious past through throwaway comment.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: This tastes like my first marriage smells.

jokes.text: David's reactions: 'Don't need that' and 'Ew' to parent analogies
jokes.setup: Moira makes sexual/romantic analogies about parents
jokes.punchline: David's disgusted interruptions
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beats showing David's discomfort with parent oversharing.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Your father and I are like two puzzle pieces that fit together perfectly. He's the dong, and I'm the—

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm going to stop you right there.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: —vag.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yeah, I got the gist of it, and I didn't need you to complete that sentence.

jokes.text: This feels remarkably selfless. - You and Patrick are two good grapes.
jokes.setup: David commenting on Moira's apparent generosity
jokes.punchline: Moira immediately launching into wine metaphors about his relationship
jokes.explanation: David's suspicion proves correct as Moira immediately makes it about relationship advice. Wine metaphor is perfectly Moira.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mother, that's very generous of you.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, darling, a relationship is like a fine wine. It requires proper aging, the right temperature, and someone to keep it away from your husband.

jokes.text: Johnny's terrible small talk about concession prices
jokes.setup: Johnny continues avoiding real conversation with Patrick
jokes.punchline: Desperately grasps at baseball-related small talk
jokes.explanation: Continues awkward avoidance theme with increasingly desperate topic choices.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: So... did you catch the game last night?

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Which game?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Any game. All games. I don't know, I wasn't really paying attention.

jokes.text: Patrick's list of things that set David off: 'And there are many, many things that can set him off'
jokes.setup: Patrick promises to protect David from life's difficulties
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges David's extensive sensitivity list
jokes.explanation: Good callback to David's established character trait while showing Patrick's understanding acceptance.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I'm going to protect you from life's difficulties.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's very sweet, but I have an extensive list of sensitivities.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I'm aware.

jokes.text: Okay, speaking of Herbert, is it Herbert or Erbert? Like when people say Herb or Erb?
jokes.setup: They're in the middle of a wine disaster
jokes.punchline: David getting distracted by pronunciation questions at the worst moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect David moment - getting distracted by linguistic minutiae during a crisis. Shows his priorities and anxiety patterns.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Is it 'Pinot Noir' or 'Pinot Nwar'? Because I've been saying it wrong this entire time and now I'm having an existential crisis about my credibility in the wine community.

jokes.text: 'Our designated grapes!' when Johnny arrives
jokes.setup: Johnny arrives to drive drunk wife and son home
jokes.punchline: They continue grape metaphor even while drunk
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier metaphor while showing their continued drunkenness.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm not drunk, I'm just a little... fermented.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, you can barely stand.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, a fine wine needs time to breathe, David. And I am breathing... extensively.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Let's get you both in the car.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Why are we still doing the grape thing?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Because I am a vintage, and vintage only improves with age and... intoxication.

jokes.text: 'I am offering a major apology if that'll help' followed by awkward silence
jokes.setup: Johnny tries to salvage business relationship
jokes.punchline: Herb's complete rejection creates uncomfortable silence
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Johnny's desperate attempt at damage control.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Herb, I think we should talk about moving forward together. I have some ideas that could really benefit both our businesses.

dialogue.speaker: Herb
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm sorry?

dialogue.speaker: Herb
dialogue.line: No. The answer is no. It was no yesterday, it's no today, and it will be no tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, I think if you just heard me out—

dialogue.speaker: Herb
dialogue.line: I don't need to hear you out, Johnny. I've made my decision.

jokes.text: I am offering a major apology if that'll help.
jokes.setup: Herb angrily asking them to leave
jokes.punchline: Moira's drunken attempt at damage control
jokes.explanation: Drunk Moira's formal but ineffective attempt to salvage the business relationship

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Herb, darling, I think we got off on the wrong foot. You see, I'm actually a very spiritual person. I have a spirit animal.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: It's a flamingo. Very majestic. Very... pink.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: And I feel like if you just got to know my flamingo, you'd understand me better.

jokes.text: Stevie revealing she knew it was a cult all along
jokes.setup: David accuses Stevie of exposing Alexis to cult
jokes.punchline: Stevie casually reveals she knew from the beginning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie reveal - she was aware all along but went along anyway.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You exposed Alexis to a cult?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah, I knew from the beginning.

jokes.text: David's 'twin brothers who kiss' complaint about sharing room
jokes.setup: David is uncomfortable with Patrick and Johnny bonding
jokes.punchline: Creates specific, disturbing metaphor for their relationship
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and disturbing metaphor that's perfectly David - dramatic and slightly inappropriate.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's like watching my husband and my father fall in love with each other, and I'm just supposed to be happy about it. It's deeply unsettling.

jokes.text: Now, do the step machines actually lift off the ground, or are the step machines just there to help you practice for when you walk onto the spaceship?
jokes.setup: David learning about Alexis's cult experience
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact questions about the logistics of cult space travel
jokes.explanation: David's practical approach to absurd cult logistics. His serious tone about spaceships is perfectly deadpan.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: So when you say you were in a cult, do you mean like a... small group of people, or...?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: It was called the Celestial Beings. We believed we were going to be transported to another dimension.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: And how exactly were you planning to get there? Because I have to imagine the logistics of that would be—

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: We were going to ascend.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Right, but like, did you need to book anything? Or was it more of a... walk situation?

jokes.text: Anyone think I might like a sleepover? - Ew... Ew!
jokes.setup: Roland wanting to join the family gathering
jokes.punchline: Alexis's disgusted reaction to Roland's presence
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective disgust reaction to Roland's inappropriate suggestion. Alexis's double 'ew' sells it.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Can I join you folks for dinner?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, no.

tmdb_overview: Moira and David sample wine at Herb Ertlinger's vineyard, while Johnny attempts to have “the talk” with Patrick.
insight: Moira delivers 50 jokes in 44 minutes—her verbal gymnastics sustain mid-tier laughs without a standout peak.
